changes.reason: Batch G first pass should risk-box the weakest no-0DTE fallback without touching VALID_0DTE profiles. STALE_0DTE breakout_no_0dte had only 2 trades, $71 PnL, 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, 52.6 minute median hold, and -250.25 worst MAE; requiring stronger ES VWAP slope tests whether stale fallback needs cleaner directional momentum.

Raise the stale/expired fallback breakout structure requirement from 4 to 5 to avoid no-0DTE entries that rely on weaker ES structure when option evidence is unavailable or stale. This stays within the missing-0DTE adapted family and avoids option-dependent profiles.

Current breakout_no_0dte uses 3 points but realized stale/expired fallback median TP was 6, with the EXPIRED_0DTE trade producing high MFE and high efficiency. Testing a 4 point fixed target should reduce churn and better align fallback entries with stronger ES-only continuation while remaining less aggressive than the observed 6 point dynamic target.

Pullback_no_0dte stale trades are positive but low efficiency at 83.2 PnL/exposure hour. Raising structure from 4 to 5 is a narrow fallback-only quality test intended to keep stale fallback trades only when ES structure is unambiguous.

expected_effect: Should improve fallback MAE and PnL per exposure hour by making STALE_0DTE/EXPIRED_0DTE ES-only participation more selective, while preserving VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE performance. Main tradeoff is possible loss of a few positive stale fallback trades; reject if total net profit retention falls below 98%, fallback expectancy turns negative, or trade count drops more than 10% without a material risk-adjusted improvement.
label: fallback-es-quality-box
rationale: Follow the required run order with Batch G using only routed fallback profiles. The evidence says missing-state routing is working, but STALE_0DTE fallback is the lowest-efficiency slice: 5 trades, $162.8 total PnL, with breakout_long_no_0dte especially tail-heavy and slow. This recommendation does not tune option-confirmed profiles for missing/stale states and does not use unsupported missing-0DTE policy keys. It tests whether stronger ES-only confirmation can contain stale fallback exposure without damaging the core VALID_0DTE edge.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributed only $162.8 over 5 trades; breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades had -210.6 average MAE, -250.25 worst MAE, 52.6 minute median hold, and only 40.5 PnL/exposure hour.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Stale 0DTE Defensive Fallback
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Sample is small, so tighter fallback structure may overfit and remove positive stale trades without improving full-sample objective.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: 0DTE state is STALE_0DTE or EXPIRED_0DTE and routing uses ES-only no-0DTE fallback profiles.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Fallback average MAE improves and fallback PnL/exposure hour rises while total net profit remains at least 98% of run_001 and VALID_0DTE metrics are unchanged.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: The run is a short-sample, long-only, strong-upside regime with 100% realized win rate, no stop losses, no slow 12h holds, and low portfolio drawdown, but the path data shows th...

new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Clean Pullback Split
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and average MAE by at least 25% while retaining at least 70% of its PnL and no slow 12h trades.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Stale 0DTE Defensive Fallback
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Improve fallback PnL/exposure hour and reduce fallback avg MAE while preserving positive fallback PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Fallback Discovery
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Any activation must beat breakout_long_1dte on average MAE and worst MAE while maintaining positive PnL/exposure hour.
